final_test_output:
1	#!/bin/bash
     2	set -e
     3	
     4	# Start Redis
     5	redis-server --daemonize yes --loglevel warning
     6	sleep 1
     7	
     8	# Start the app in the background
     9	cd /app
    10	node app/server.js &
    11	APP_PID=$!
    12	sleep 2
    13	
    14	# Wait for the server to be ready
    15	for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    16	    if curl -s http://localhost:3000/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    17	        break
    18	    fi
    19	    sleep 0.5
    20	done
    21	
    22	# Reset the counter
    23	curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/counter/reset > /dev/null
    24	
    25	# Verify reset worked
    26	INITIAL=$(curl -s http://localhost:3000/counter | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['value'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "error")
    27	if [ "$INITIAL" != "0" ]; then
    28	    echo "FAIL: Counter did not reset to 0, got $INITIAL"
    29	    kill $APP_PID 2>/dev/null || true
    30	    exit 1
    31	fi
    32	
    33	# Fire 100 concurrent increments
    34	CONCURRENCY=100
    35	echo "Firing $CONCURRENCY concurrent increments..."
    36	
    37	CURL_PIDS=""
    38	for i in $(seq 1 $CONCURRENCY); do
    39	    curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/counter/increment > /dev/null &
    40	    CURL_PIDS="$CURL_PIDS $!"
    41	done
    42	
    43	# Wait for all background curl processes to finish
    44	for pid in $CURL_PIDS; do
    45	    wait $pid 2>/dev/null || true
    46	done
    47	
    48	# Small delay to ensure all writes have landed
    49	sleep 1
    50	
    51	# Read the final counter value
    52	FINAL=$(curl -s http://localhost:3000/counter | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['value'])")
    53	
    54	echo "Expected: $CONCURRENCY"
    55	echo "Got:      $FINAL"
    56	
    57	# Cleanup
    58	kill $APP_PID 2>/dev/null || true
    59	
    60	if [ "$FINAL" = "$CONCURRENCY" ]; then
    61	    echo "PASS: Counter is correct"
    62	    exit 0
    63	else
    64	    echo "FAIL: Counter should be $CONCURREN
